data.narrative:
Blackshark.ai, a Defense & Security software provider, is being valued at Series A stage against a comp universe of 10 private firms and 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector.

Two valuation methods triangulate the fair value range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $55M in total capital raised, implying $110M. Peer-median analysis draws from four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M)—yielding a median valuation of $225M with a range of $125M to $500M. The dispersion reflects meaningful variance in scale and positioning within the early-stage defense tech market.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $94M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $168M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively; the peer-median approach reflects actual recent market transactions and commands a substantially higher midpoint. Key uncertainties include whether Blackshark.ai's technology differentiation, revenue trajectory, and customer concentration align with the higher-valuation peers (Stark Defence, Vatn Systems) or the lower-valuation subset. Additional clarity on recurring revenue, customer concentration, and go-to-market momentum would refine placement within this range.
